question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here are all the hook events a Juju charm can observe:

## Lifecycle Hooks
- **install** — runs once when the unit is first deployed
- **config-changed** — runs when configuration is changed (and after install)
- **start** — runs after install and config-changed
- **stop** — runs when the unit is being destroyed
- **remove** — runs when the unit is being removed
- **upgrade-charm** — runs when the charm is upgraded

## Relation Hooks
For each relation defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-created** — a new relation is established
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-joined** — a new unit joins the relation
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-changed** — relation data is updated
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-departed** — a unit leaves the relation
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-broken** — the relation is fully removed

## Storage Hooks
For each storage defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<storage-name\>-storage-attached** — storage is attached to the unit
- **\<storage-name\>-storage-detaching** — storage is about to be detached

## Container/Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes charms only)
For each container defined in `charmcraft.yaml`:
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-ready** — Pebble is ready in the container
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-custom-notice** — a custom Pebble notice fires
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-check-failed** — a Pebble health check fails
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-check-recovered** — a Pebble health check recovers

## Leadership Hooks
- **leader-elected** — this unit has become the leader
- **leader-settings-changed** — leader settings have changed (legacy)

## Action Hooks
- **\<action-name\>-action** — an action is invoked by the user

## Secret Hooks
- **secret-changed** — a tracked secret has a new revision
- **secret-expired** — a secret owned by this charm has expired
- **secret-rotate** — a secret owned by this charm needs rotation
- **secret-remove** — an old secret revision can be removed

## Other
- **update-status** — fires periodically (default every 5 minutes)
- **collect-unit-status** — called before status is reported (unit)
- **collect-app-status** — called before status is reported (app)
- **pre-series-upgrade** / **post-series-upgrade** — OS series upgrade (legacy, now replaced by machine upgrade hooks)

In Ops, these are observed as Python events on `self.on`, e.g. `self.on.install`, `self.on.config_changed`, `self.on[relation_name].relation_joined`, `self.on[container_name].pebble_ready`, etc.
